name: Python: Compose agent implementation — 34 files, 51 hunks
description: Real-world diff: implements the Compose Agent feature with 27 new files and 7 modified files. Includes language-specific symbol extractors (13 extractor files inheriting from base.py), agent orchestrator, dependency graph, checkpoint validator, programmatic grouping, LLM messenger, cache infrastructure (paths, save/load, metadata model), CLI integration (--agent/--graph/--trace flags), compose __init__.py re-exports, and 7 test files. Key challenges: (1) models.py is a hub imported by 16+ modules, (2) all 12 extractors inherit from base.py, (3) cache/__init__.py re-exports from BOTH paths.py AND compose.py — all three must be co-committed, (4) cli/compose.py imports save_compose_agent_trace from hunknote.cache (the package __init__.py), so the entire cache subsystem (paths.py + compose.py + __init__.py) must be committed BEFORE cli/compose.py, (5) compose/__init__.py re-exports from all compose submodules so it must come after them, (6) strict ordering: models before consumers, base before ALL extractors, source before tests, agent before CLI. Derived from checkpoint failure analysis: commits C1–C6 broke with ImportError because cli/compose.py was committed before cache/__init__.py re-exported save_compose_agent_trace. The ideal plan separates: data models, extractor subsystem, graph/symbols/checkpoint core, grouping/agent, cache infrastructure, CLI integration, and tests — respecting dependency order.
